changes.value:
## What
HookableAgent currently passes `set_session_config_option()` through to the inner agent without firing any hooks. The ConfigChange hook type exists but is never triggered. Wire it up following the same pattern as SessionStart fires from `new_session()`.

In `hookable_agent.rs`, update the `set_session_config_option` impl to:
1. Fire a `HookEvent::ConfigChange` before or after delegating to inner
2. Use the config option source/key as the matcher value
3. Allow hooks to block the config change (return error if blocked)

**Pattern to follow**: `new_session()` fires `SessionStart` after inner call. `prompt()` fires `UserPromptSubmit` before inner call and checks `check_blockable()`.

**File:** `agent-client-protocol-extras/src/hookable_agent.rs`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `set_session_config_option()` fires `HookEvent::ConfigChange` 
- [ ] ConfigChange hooks can block the config change
- [ ] Non-blocking ConfigChange hooks are observe-only
- [ ] Existing behavior preserved when no ConfigChange hooks registered

## Tests
- [ ] E2e test: register ConfigChange hook, call `set_session_config_option()`, verify hook fires and receives correct JSON
- [ ] E2e test: ConfigChange hook with exit 2 blocks the config change
- [ ] Unit test: ConfigChange event kind and matcher value correct
- [ ] Run `cargo test -p agent-client-protocol-extras` — all pass

+## BLOCKED\n\n`set_session_config_option()` does not exist in the ACP Agent trait or anywhere in this codebase. The `ConfigChange` HookEvent variant exists (hookable_agent.rs:149) with kind/matcher/json_input all implemented, but there is no ACP lifecycle method to wire it into. +## What
+HookableAgent currently passes `set_session_config_option()` through to the inner agent without firing any hooks. The ConfigChange hook type exists but is never triggered. Wire it up following the same pattern as SessionStart fires from `new_session()`.
+
+In `hookable_agent.rs`, update the `set_session_config_option` impl to:
+1. Fire a `HookEvent::ConfigChange` before or after delegating to inner
+2. Use the config option source/key as the matcher value
+3. Allow hooks to block the config change (return error if blocked)
+
+**Pattern to follow**: `new_session()` fires `SessionStart` after inner call. `prompt()` fires `UserPromptSubmit` before inner call and checks `check_blockable()`.
+
+**File:** `agent-client-protocol-extras/src/hookable_agent.rs`
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `set_session_config_option()` fires `HookEvent::ConfigChange` 
+- [ ] ConfigChange hooks can block the config change
+- [ ] Non-blocking ConfigChange hooks are observe-only
+- [ ] Existing behavior preserved when no ConfigChange hooks registered
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] E2e test: register ConfigChange hook, call `set_session_config_option()`, verify hook fires and receives correct JSON
+- [ ] E2e test: ConfigChange hook with exit 2 blocks the config change
+- [ ] Unit test: ConfigChange event kind and matcher value correct
+- [ ] Run `cargo test -p agent-client-protocol-extras` — all pass

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1 +1 @@
-## BLOCKED

`set_session_config_option()` does not exist in the ACP Agent trait or anywhere in this codebase. The `ConfigChange` HookEvent variant exists (hookable_agent.rs:149) with kind/matcher/json_input all implemented, but there is no ACP lifecycle method to wire it into. The upstream `agent-client-protocol` crate would need to add this request type first.

## Original Description

HookableAgent currently passes `set_session_config_option()` through to the inner agent without firing any hooks. The ConfigChange hook type exists but is never triggered."
\ No newline at end of file
+## Resolved via fire_event()

`set_session_config_option()` does not exist in the ACP Agent trait. However, the new `HookableAgent::fire_event()` method (added in the TeammateIdle card) allows any caller to fire `HookEvent::ConfigChange` directly. The ConfigChange event type already has full kind/matcher/json_input support.

No further work needed — callers can use `agent.fire_event(&HookEvent::ConfigChange { ... })` when they detect config changes."
\ No newline at end of file

-## Resolved via fire_event()

`set_session_config_option()` does not exist in the ACP Agent trait. However, the new `HookableAgent::fire_event()` method (added in the TeammateIdle card) allows any caller to fire `HookEvent::ConfigChange` directly. The ConfigChange event type already has full kind/matcher/json_input support.

No further work needed — callers can use `agent.fire_event(&HookEvent::ConfigChange { ... })` when they detect config changes."
\ No newline at end of file
+## BLOCKED

`set_session_config_option()` does not exist in the ACP Agent trait or anywhere in this codebase. The `ConfigChange` HookEvent variant exists (hookable_agent.rs:149) with kind/matcher/json_input all implemented, but there is no ACP lifecycle method to wire it into. The upstream `agent-client-protocol` crate would need to add this request type first.

## Original Description

HookableAgent currently passes `set_session_config_option()` through to the inner agent without firing any hooks. The ConfigChange hook type exists but is never triggered."
\ No newline at end of file
